################################################################################
# batch_ao.txt — gameplay C1->C2 drain (campaign batch 4 of ~5)
#
# Generated by the discover-c1-batch skill, 2026-06-03.
# Work type:    promote-c2 (C1 -> C2 mechanical promotion). AUTHOR-ONLY fanout.
# Sessions:     6  (Mashed_pool1,2,9,10,12,15 pre-assigned)
#               -> pool0,4,5,6 SKIPPED: leaked in-JVM locks during prior fanouts
#                  (see memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock).
#               -> pool7,8 SKIPPED: POISONED during batch_an (an_s4 leaked an in-JVM
#                  lock on pool7 after a missing-program_name open; an_s5 hit a
#                  LockException on pool8). They may stay poisoned for the shared
#                  server's lifetime — do NOT re-assign without re-verification.
#               -> pool3,10,11,13,14 carry STALE on-disk .lock/.lock~ from batch_an
#                  read-only opens, but pool10 opened read-only cleanly for an_s6 and
#                  pool3/11/14 for an_s3/s5/s4. A stale on-disk lock is tolerable;
#                  test-open read-only and fall back only on a real LockException.
# Total RVAs:   156  (gameplay, next-156 by address 0x0047f450..0x00562380)
# Model:        Opus 4.8 (1M context)  (claude-opus-4-8[1m])
#               -> promote-c2 bulk-drain cap 60 RVAs/session (this batch: 26 each).
#               Same author-only cadence as batch_aj/ak/al/am/an (Opus author-only
#               fanout + scripted ghidra-sweep + central re-classify).
#
# CAMPAIGN CONTEXT: gameplay is now a 324-RVA C1 pool (was 792; al -> 636, am -> 480,
# an -> 324). This is batch 4 of ~5. It takes the NEXT 156 by address above
# 0x0047f380. After this slice 168 gameplay-C1 rows remain (the final batch 5/~5).
#
# *** READ THIS — THIS SLICE IS NOT LIKE batch_al/am/an. ***
# al/am/an drained the 0x00405-0x0047f application-code pages (race/entity/effect
# logic), where "gameplay" was mostly right and reclass went to render/audio/hud.
# batch_ao crosses into the RenderWare engine pages. The gameplay-C1 rows that
# remain above 0x0047f380 are DOMINATED by vendored / engine code that was
# mislabeled "gameplay" in hooks.csv:
#   * 0x004c0xxx-0x004c4xxx  = RenderWare 3.x CORE math/frame/matrix/atomic
#     (e.g. FUN_004c3ac0 = Vec3Magnitude is a LANDED hook; 004c1xxx = RpMaterial/
#     transform setters; 004c5xxx = RwFrame ops). third-party-library[renderware].
#   * 0x004e4xxx-0x004e8xxx  = RenderWare RpClump / RwFrame CORE
#     (CONFIRMED in batch_an plates: FUN_004e45b0 = RwFrameRemoveChild,
#     FUN_004e6e00 = RpClumpDestroy). third-party-library[renderware].
#   * 0x00554xxx-0x00562xxx  = RenderWare-Physics-3.7 + RWS/geometry engine code,
#     INTERLEAVED with the qhull island just above (0x0057c5b0+). batch_aj already
#     reclassed 0055dc70/00562520 -> RW-Physics; batch_ak reclassed 00559c40/
#     0055ae70/b940/bab0/c4a0 -> RW-Physics. EXPECT many more here.
# CONSEQUENCE: EXPECT A HIGH library_skip / reclass-OUT RATE. Many candidates are
# NOT C1->C2 promotions — they are vendored engine functions that must be
# reclassed-OUT to third-party-library[renderware] / [RenderWare-Physics-3.7] and
# KEPT C1 (library residue, not reimplementation targets — same as batch_ai s4's
# 47 reclassed rows and the batch_ak tail's 14). Either way they LEAVE gameplay C1,
# so the campaign still drains; the C2-promotion count will simply be lower than 156.
#
# HOW TO DECIDE per RVA (library-confirm rule — apply it AGGRESSIVELY this batch):
#   - If the function decodes as a named RenderWare/CRT/qhull primitive, OR Ghidra
#     shows a "Library Function: <sym>" tag, OR it is an obvious thin engine
#     primitive (matrix mul, frame add/remove-child, clump/atomic create/destroy,
#     geometry lock/unlock, vector normalize) with NO game-state read/write:
#       -> do NOT plate it as C1->C2. Put it in the queue row's
#          `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>` field (tag = renderware | RenderWare-Physics-3.7
#          | msvc-crt | qhull-2002.1). Do NOT rename it on master. Central
#          re-classify reclasses it to third-party-library[<tag>] and keeps it C1.
#   - Only GENUINE game/engine-GLUE that reads/writes game state goes in `rvas=`
#     for the C1->C2 plate. When in doubt about library-vs-glue, plate it C1->C2 and
#     record subsystem_observed + an [UNCERTAIN] — do NOT silently drop it.
#
# Pre-flight (verified at generation time, 2026-06-03):
#   * re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Queued = EMPTY. batch_an fully swept + re-classified
#     (sweep-20260603-1259; commits 42f14271 drain + 080bdfb3 release + 5d52d8f3
#     re-classify). gameplay C1 480 -> 324. SAFE to fan out. (If a sweep is running
#     when you start, STOP and re-confirm.)
#   * Calibrated-band screen over all 156 candidates: ZERO hits in the three
#     CALIBRATED bands (CRT 0x004a0000-0x004b3fff | D3DX9-PSGP 0x004ec000-0x004fc9e0
#     | qhull 0x0057c5b0-0x005a5820). BUT see the RenderWare/RW-Physics warning above
#     — those vendored functions live OUTSIDE the calibrated bands and are caught by
#     the per-RVA library-confirm rule, not by an address screen.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHOR-ONLY PROTOCOL (read before any session runs) — identical to batch_aj/ak/al/am/an
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   * DO write per-RVA .md plates into the session's bucket dir, using the
#     template heading **`## Mechanical description`** (NOT `## Purpose` — batch_aj
#     s3 used `## Purpose` and the sweep manifest builder returned 0 plated for
#     that bucket until renamed. This heading is a HARD requirement.)
#   * DO append ONE row to a PER-SESSION queue fragment:
#         re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s<N>.md   (NOT the shared re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md)
#     The queued row MUST use the field name **`rvas=`** for everything the sweep
#     should plate (NOT `rvas_c2=` — batch_ak s3 used `rvas_c2=` and the builder
#     could not see it). List vendored ones in a SEPARATE
#     `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,...` field for the central re-classify (they are NOT
#     plated by the sweep). EXPECT this field to be non-empty this batch.
#   * DO NOT write hooks.csv. DO NOT run re-classify. DO NOT touch STUBS.md /
#     UNCERTAINTIES.md / DEFERRED.md / re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md directly.
#   * DO NOT mutate the master Ghidra project. Read-only MCP only (function_at,
#     decomp_function, listing_*). function_create / renames are the SWEEP's job —
#     if an RVA has no function object, write the plate from the raw listing and
#     set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row.
#   * DO NOT mint U-IDs or S-IDs. Mark new holes inline as bare `[UNCERTAIN]` in
#     the plate's `## Uncertainties` section with the gap described; central
#     re-classify mints from the reserved range (this batch: U-8600..U-8899,
#     S-6800..S-6999).
#   * Each session reads ONLY its pre-assigned pool clone (read-only). One commit
#     at end of session (plates + queue fragment), message:
#         "scribe(batch_ao s<N>): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay <range>"
#     Commit ATOMICALLY and path-scoped: `git commit -- <your bucket dir> <your
#     fragment>` — do NOT `git add -A`/`git add .` (a sibling parallel session may
#     have staged unrelated files; see memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #5,
#     the batch_aj-s5 shared-working-copy index race).
#
# DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #7):
#   Before doing ANY work, check whether your bucket dir / fragment / pool marker
#   already exists (another terminal launched the same session). If so, STOP and
#   yield — do NOT overwrite. If you already wrote an early file that the owner
#   keeps, yield by `git restore`, NEVER blind-`rm`.
#
# FUZZY-GREP GOTCHA (memory; seen in am_s5 + an_s6):
#   A naive `grep <rva> hooks.csv` fuzzy-matches your RVA to unrelated C2 rows that
#   merely CITE it in the notes column. Confirm each candidate's OWN row with an
#   ANCHORED grep `^<rva>,` before deciding it is/ isn't drift.
#
# FOLLOW-UP (single Opus session on main, AFTER all 6 commit — do NOT run now):
#   Scripted ghidra-sweep:
#     1. py -3.12 scripts/sweep_build_manifest.py --rows re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s1.md
#        ... re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s6.md --out sweep_manifest.json --date <D> --level C2
#        (confirm per-bucket plated == queued `rvas=` counts AND missing_md==0; note
#        the plated total will be < 156 if library_skip is used — that is expected).
#     2. ghidra_eval apply via scripts/ghidra/sweep_apply_eval.py (one txn).
#     3. Central re-classify on main: C1->C2 for plated RVAs; honor subsystem_observed
#        reclassifications; reclass-OUT every library_skip RVA to
#        third-party-library[<tag>] (kept-C1); mint U-/S- IDs from the reserved ranges.
#        Merge fragments into SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL-SLOT HYGIENE (every session)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ao_s<N>"      # per-session marker, no race
#   Use the PRE-ASSIGNED Mashed_pool<S> clone directly (do NOT call
#   ghidra_pool.ps1 acquire). pool0/4/5/6 + pool7/8 are SKIPPED (see header). If your
#   assigned slot throws a real LockException, fall back to ANOTHER verified-free
#   clone (NOT pool0/4/5/6/7/8; try pool3/11/13/14 — stale-lock-tolerant — then any
#   fresh clone), and record the ACTUAL slot used in the queue row.
#   ALWAYS program_close at end.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBSYSTEM HINT (not a claim)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Working hypothesis for all 156: gameplay (their current hooks.csv label). Per the
#   warning above, EXPECT a large share to be RenderWare / RW-Physics vendored code
#   (-> library_skip, kept C1) and a smaller share to reclass to render/audio/etc.
#   CONFIRM per RVA from decomp and record subsystem_observed=<X> / library_skip in
#   the queue row. The sweep + central re-classify reconcile.
################################################################################


================================================================================
Session 1 — bucket_gameplay_0047f450_004e4440  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool1 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0047f450_004e4440/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s1.md  (per-session fragment; sweep merges later)

# Mission
First slice of the gameplay batch-4 pool. Span 0x0047f450-0x004e4440: a 0x0047f
+ 0x00489 application tail, THEN a jump into the RenderWare-core 0x004c0xxx-0x004c4xxx
and 0x004e4xxx pages (math/frame/clump primitives). Read each end-to-end. The
0x0047f/0x00489 rows are likely genuine game glue (C1->C2 plate); the 0x004c/0x004e
rows are likely RenderWare primitives (-> library_skip=<rva>:renderware, kept C1).
Apply the library-confirm rule per RVA. Confirm/reclassify each; do NOT silently drop.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA: read the path in that RVA's hooks.csv `file` column
  FIRST (anchored `^<rva>,` grep — see fuzzy-grep gotcha). Deepen, don't restart.
  Drift-skip any already >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Session 2 (next range).

# Workflow
1. DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (header). Then export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ao_s1";
   use Mashed_pool1 read-only.
2. For each RVA: function_at -> decomp_function (-> listing_code_unit_at if no fn;
   set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row if no function object).
3. Apply the library-confirm rule. For a genuine-glue RVA: write
   re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0047f450_004e4440/<rva>.md with frontmatter
   (rva, name, size_bytes, confidence_target=C2, callees_depth1, callers_noted,
   opened_in_slot, session_date=2026-06-03) + "## Mechanical description" +
   "## Constants" table + "## Uncertainties" (bare [UNCERTAIN], do NOT mint) +
   "## Stubs encountered" (note, do NOT mint). For a vendored RVA: do NOT plate;
   add it to library_skip=<rva>:<tag> in the queue row.
4. Append ONE row to re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s1.md: date, ao_s1, bucket, rvas=<csv of
   PLATED only>, confidence=C1->C2, note=<subsystem_observed + reclassifications +
   library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,... + needs_function_create=... + actual pool slot>.
5. One atomic path-scoped commit: "scribe(batch_ao s1): author <K> C1->C2 plates
   for gameplay 0047f450_004e4440".

# Candidates (26)
0047f450,0047f480,0047f6d0,0047fad0,004893a0,004893b0,00489450,00489480,004894a0,004894f0,00489500,00489890,004898d0,00489910,004c0790,004c0870,004c0910,004c0a60,004c0d70,004c0de0,004c1210,004c15c0,004c4220,004d8bd0,004e43b0,004e4440

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- function_at AND listing_code_unit_at both null — surface + skip.
- An RVA decodes as a library family — note in plate-or-queue, do NOT rename; set
  subsystem_observed=third-party-library[...] + library_skip=<rva>:<tag>.


================================================================================
Session 2 — bucket_gameplay_004e4800_00558030  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool2 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_004e4800_00558030/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s2.md

# Mission
Second slice. WIDE span 0x004e4800-0x00558030: RenderWare RpClump/RwFrame core
(0x004e4-0x004e8), then a jump to 0x00534 and the 0x00554-0x00558 RenderWare-
Physics / geometry pages. EXPECT this session to be HEAVY on library_skip
(renderware + RenderWare-Physics-3.7). Apply the library-confirm rule per RVA;
plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2. Confirm/reclassify each.

# Workflow
Identical to Session 1, but POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ao_s2", Mashed_pool2,
bucket dir bucket_gameplay_004e4800_00558030/, queue fragment
re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s2.md, commit "scribe(batch_ao s2): author <K> C1->C2 plates
for gameplay 004e4800_00558030".

# Candidates (26)
004e4800,004e4d90,004e6710,004e68a0,004e6920,004e6d00,004e6d80,004e6f80,004e6fe0,004e8e90,004e8ea0,00534b60,00534d00,00554010,00554150,00554200,00554390,00555830,00556780,00556e40,00557110,005572b0,005572c0,00557ec0,00557fb0,00558030

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 3 — bucket_gameplay_00558100_0055a1f0  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool9 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00558100_0055a1f0/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s3.md

# Mission
Third slice (0x00558100-0x0055a1f0), a dense RenderWare-Physics-3.7 / geometry
page. EXPECT heavy library_skip[RenderWare-Physics-3.7]. Some rows may be genuine
collision/physics GLUE that reads game state (plate those C1->C2). Apply the
library-confirm rule per RVA; confirm/reclassify each. (NB FUN_00558b40 is an
overlap-test cited by batch_an s3's detonation code — confirm whether it is engine
primitive or game glue from its body.)

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ao_s3", Mashed_pool9, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_00558100_0055a1f0/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s3.md,
commit "scribe(batch_ao s3): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay 00558100_0055a1f0".

# Candidates (26)
00558100,00558140,00558180,00558400,00558550,005586f0,00558860,00558b40,00558c50,00558c80,00558ca0,00558cb0,00558d30,00559040,005592f0,00559560,005595d0,00559b50,00559ba0,00559bc0,00559be0,00559c00,00559c20,00559ee0,0055a140,0055a1f0

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


================================================================================
Session 4 — bucket_gameplay_0055a9a0_0055bd70  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool10 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire; stale on-disk .lock is
             tolerable — an_s6 opened pool10 read-only cleanly. Fall back only on a
             real LockException.)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0055a9a0_0055bd70/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s4.md

# Mission
Fourth slice (0x0055a9a0-0x0055bd70), RenderWare-Physics-3.7 region. batch_ak
already reclassed neighbours 0055ae70/b940/bab0 -> RW-Physics — EXPECT several of
these (0055ade0/ae50/af40/af60/af70/af80/afc0/b030...) to be the same family.
Apply the library-confirm rule per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ao_s4", Mashed_pool10, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_0055a9a0_0055bd70/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s4.md,
commit "scribe(batch_ao s4): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay 0055a9a0_0055bd70".

# Candidates (26)

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.

Session 5 — bucket_gameplay_0055bd80_0055e330  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)

Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s5.md

# Mission
Fifth slice (0x0055bd80-0x0055e330), RenderWare-Physics-3.7 / geometry region.
batch_aj reclassed neighbour 0055dc70 -> RW-Physics. EXPECT heavy
library_skip[RenderWare-Physics-3.7]. Apply the library-confirm rule per RVA;
plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2. Confirm/reclassify each.

Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ao_s5", Mashed_pool12, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_0055bd80_0055e330/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s5.md,
commit "scribe(batch_ao s5): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay 0055bd80_0055e330".

================================================================================
Session 6 — bucket_gameplay_0055e440_00562380  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool15 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_0055e440_00562380/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s6.md

# Mission
Sixth slice (0x0055e440-0x00562380), the top of the RenderWare-Physics page just
below the qhull island (0x0057c5b0+). batch_aj reclassed neighbour 00562520 ->
RW-Physics. EXPECT heavy library_skip[RenderWare-Physics-3.7]. Apply the library-
confirm rule per RVA; plate only genuine game/engine-glue C1->C2. Closes batch 4/~5.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_ao_s6", Mashed_pool15, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_0055e440_00562380/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_ao_s6.md,
commit "scribe(batch_ao s6): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay 0055e440_00562380".

# Candidates (26)
0055e440,0055f450,0055f480,0055f520,0055f670,0055f800,0055fdd0,0055fe10,0055fe30,0055fe40,0055fe50,0055fea0,0055ff70,0055ff90,005601f0,00560260,00561040,00561280,00561390,00561c50,00561e60,00561e80,00561ea0,00561ec0,00562010,00562380

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


################################################################################
# END batch_ao.txt — 156 RVAs across 6 author-only sessions (gameplay campaign 4/~5).
# After all 6 commit: run the scripted ghidra-sweep on main (see header), then
# central re-classify C1->C2 + reclass-OUT every library_skip RVA to
# third-party-library[...] (kept-C1) + merge per-session queue fragments into
# re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained. gameplay C1 324 -> 168 (every candidate leaves the
# gameplay pool whether it goes C2 or library-C1; the C2-promotion count will be
# lower than 156 because of the expected high library_skip rate). Remaining after
# this slice: gameplay (~168, final batch 5/~5) + render (458).
################################################################################
